race.name: Struggle:NYM
race.tagline: Formerly Struggle Moors -- North York Moors sportive, Struggle Events series

vitals.location: Sutton Bank, North York Moors, Yorkshire, England
vitals.date_specific: Sunday, July 5, 2026 -- Sutton Bank National Park Centre, Thirsk YO7 2EH
vitals.field_size: 1,000 capacity (250 Ultra + 500 Classic + 250 Short); actual starters/finishers not verified
vitals.entry_fee: GBP49 Short / GBP59 Classic / GBP69 Ultra, plus GBP1 British Cycling fee
vitals.cutoff_time: Ultra route: 100km checkpoint by approximately 13:00 per the 2026 road book; no full cutoff schedule published for all three distances

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 59. NOT re-scored this wave -- dimensions left as inherited despite the 2026 route overhaul (130/189/250km replacing the old single-distance format) and rebrand to Struggle:NYM.

course_description.character: A real sportive in the Struggle Events series (alongside Struggle Dales and Struggle Borderlands), run by Struggle Events Limited, a Harrogate company founded by Matt and Victoria Mannakee in 2016. This event started life in 2017 as Struggle Moors -- inaugural edition July 9, 2017, from Ampleforth Abbey, roughly 174 km with an estimated field of 1,000 -- and for 2026 the organizer rebranded it Struggle:NYM (North York Moors); its own materials explicitly call it 'formerly Struggle Moors.' The 2026 edition, held July 5 from Sutton Bank National Park Centre, offered three distances: a 130 km Short (1,957 m), a 189 km Classic (3,144 m), and a 250 km Ultra (3,906 m) -- an expansion from 2025's two-distance format (100/161 km, per British Cycling's 2025 listing). The organizer's own website is internally inconsistent in places (some sections still display stale '165KM' and '120KM' labels alongside correctly updated 189/130 km body copy); the final rider road book and British Cycling's listing agree on 130/189/250 km, and those are the figures to trust.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Ultra route's 3,906 m of climbing across 250 km and ten named climbs.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Options in nearby North Yorkshire towns such as Thirsk, Helmsley, or Harrogate, or within the North York Moors National Park.

history.origin_story: Launched July 9, 2017 as Struggle Moors, from Ampleforth Abbey, by Struggle Events Limited -- the Harrogate-based promoter (founded 2016) also behind Struggle Dales and Struggle Borderlands, created in the wake of the 2014 Tour de France Grand Depart in Yorkshire. Rebranded Struggle:NYM for 2026 with a new start venue (Sutton Bank) and an overhauled three-distance route structure, delivered in partnership with the North York Moors National Park Authority.

biased_opinion.summary: Struggle Moors is a real, standalone sportive in the Struggle Events series (alongside Struggle Dales and Struggle Borderlands), run by Struggle Events Limited out of Harrogate since 2016. It is not a route option within some larger single event -- it has its own inaugural date (July 9, 2017, Ampleforth Abbey) and its own edition history. For 2026, the organizer rebranded it Struggle:NYM (North York Moors), moved the start to Sutton Bank, and expanded from 2025's two-distance format (100/161 km) to three distances (130/189/250 km). The organizer's own web pages haven't fully caught up with the rebrand -- parts of the site still carry stale distance labels from the old format -- but the final rider road book and British Cycling's listing agree on the current 130/189/250 km figures.
strengths: A real, currently active event with a genuine multi-year track record (2017-2026) and an identifiable organizer (Struggle Events Limited, Harrogate)
A serious 2026 route overhaul gives riders a genuine choice of three distances rather than one fixed course, from a 130km short option up to a 250km/3,906m Ultra
The North York Moors National Park Authority's own records confirm a partnership with Struggle Events to deliver cycling events from Sutton Bank
weaknesses: The 2026 rebrand to 'Struggle:NYM' means riders searching for 'Struggle Moors' may not immediately recognize this as the same event -- and the organizer's own site still displays stale distance labels in places (e.g. '165KM' and '120KM' headings next to correctly updated 189/130km body text), which is sloppy presentation for a paid entry
No verified 2027 date at time of review -- the product page floats a return 'in 2027' but that's marketing language, not a confirmed edition

An official results portal exists for 2026 (my.raceresult.com/408444), but finisher/starter totals weren't independently confirmed from indexed results at time of review -- the 1,000-rider figure is advertised capacity, not verified attendance
The 2026 road book does specify at least one intermediate cutoff (Ultra route: 100km checkpoint by roughly 13:00), but no full published cutoff schedule for all three distances was found
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a genuinely tough, well-organized North York Moors sportive with real distance options up to 250km/3,906m, this delivers -- just look for it under 'Struggle:NYM' now, and cross-check the current road book rather than trusting the organizer's own web copy on exact distances.

final_verdict.one_liner: North York Moors sportive, rebranded Struggle:NYM for 2026, with a genuinely tough 250km/3,906m Ultra option.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you want a legitimate, organized North York Moors sportive with real distance choice (130/189/250km) and don't mind that it's no longer marketed as 'Struggle Moors.' Check the current road book for exact figures before committing -- the organizer's own site hasn't fully caught up with its own 2026 rebrand.
final_verdict.alternatives: Struggle Dales or Struggle Borderlands (same organizer, different Yorkshire regions) for the wider series; the Exmoor Epic/Hardest Hundred (Exmoor National Park, Devon/Somerset) for a comparably brutal English climbing sportive outside Yorkshire.

catalog_flags.status_note: Rebrand notice: the organizer renamed this event 'Struggle:NYM' for 2026 (formerly Struggle Moors), moved the start to Sutton Bank, and replaced the old route structure with three new distances (130/189/250km). Kept race.name/display_name updated to the current branding with the former name preserved in tagline/history for continuity; slug left unchanged (struggle-moors) to avoid breaking existing URLs.

youtube_data.search_query: Struggle Moors gran fondo cycling North York Moors

videos.title: Chilled Cafe Ride and Boltby Bank!
videos.description:
Episode 18 - Join me on a chilled Sunday social cafe ride, then I tackle some more brutal Yorkshire hills, including Boltby Bank. One tough hill, made even harder by the hilly approach and tricky surface on the climb in parts, as you will see! Worth it for the views though....

Link to my ride - https://www.strava.com/activities/8901504344

Struggle event which I've entered - https://ridethestruggle.com/products/struggle-moors-hardest-sportive-in-yorkshire  

Please Like and Subscribe to the channel for more regular videos.

Follow me:
Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/cyclingunleashed/
Strava - https://www.strava.com/athletes/21407791
Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/cyclingunleashed

Credit to Videezy.com for the video overlays
videos.curation_reason: First-person ride experience specifically training for Struggle Moors event with detailed commentary on North Yorkshire terrain including Boltby Bank.

quotes.text: I think I'm also going to sign up for the struggle event that's in four weeks... I thought it was a much better idea to get out and do some miles get a decent riding get some hills in today as a bit of training for that.
This is a lot much fun, it's never a good time when you can't even stop going down it, breaks pretty much on full... Jesus, where's the road go.

rider_intel.search_text: A rider training for the Struggle Moors/Struggle:NYM sportive describes a North Yorkshire training ride tackling Boltby Bank, a brutal local climb made harder by the hilly approach and tricky surface in parts. The climb produced one of the toughest efforts the rider had done, with steep, straight sections that offer no relief once the gradient kicks in. This is first-person training footage for the event, not official race coverage.

eligibility.notes: Confirmed real and currently active. Rebranded 'Struggle:NYM' for 2026 (organizer's own copy says 'previously Struggle Moors'); 2026 edition held July 5 from Sutton Bank National Park Centre with a new 130/189/250km route structure, confirmed via the official road book and British Cycling's 2026 registration listing. No confirmed 2027 date/entry found as of this review; the organizer's own marketing floats a 2027 return without a firm date.
